does: This paper introduces Audio MultiChallenge, an open-source benchmark for evaluating end-to-end spoken dialogue systems on multi-turn conversational abilities using natural human speech. The benchmark extends the text-based MultiChallenge framework to the audio domain, assessing four key dimensions: Inference Memory (recalling prior conversation context), Instruction Retention (following previously stated directives), Self Coherence (maintaining consistency across turns), and a new audio-specific dimension called Voice Editing that tests robustness to speech repairs and backtracking. The benchmark includes 452 conversations from 47 speakers with 1,712 instance-specific evaluation rubrics, created through a hybrid pipeline combining automated audio-native agent generation with human-in-the-loop curation. Audio-Cue challenges extend Inference Memory to require systems to recall ambient sounds and paralinguistic signals beyond semantic content, reflecting realistic multimodal aspects of spoken interaction.
connects: This work builds directly on the text-based MultiChallenge framework, which established dimensions for evaluating dialogue coherence and consistency in written conversation. It responds to a significant gap in the dialogue systems evaluation literature: while cascaded speech processing pipelines dominated earlier work, recent end-to-end systems process raw audio directly, yet existing benchmarks rely heavily on synthetic speech and single-turn evaluation. Audio MultiChallenge addresses this by extending conversation quality metrics into the audio modality and multi-turn interaction patterns, combining insights from speech processing robustness studies with dialogue coherence evaluation. The work acknowledges prior research on speech repair phenomena and paralinguistic aspects of communication, incorporating these into systematic evaluation. It positions itself against limitations of current E2E spoken dialogue benchmarks that inadequately capture the complexity of natural human-AI voice interactions.
enables: This benchmark enables more rigorous development and evaluation of end-to-end spoken dialogue systems that need to operate in realistic conversational settings. By establishing systematic evaluation of multi-turn coherence, consistency, and robustness to speech phenomena in audio space, it creates infrastructure for improving E2E systems beyond single-turn synthetic speech performance. The Voice Editing dimension and Audio-Cue challenges open research directions into handling disfluencies, repairs, and paralinguistic information in dialogue. The release of 452 annotated conversations with detailed rubrics supports future work on dialogue state tracking, long-context reasoning, and acoustic grounding in dialogue systems. This work likely catalyzes development of systems that better handle natural speech variability and complex multi-turn interaction patterns, advancing the field toward more human-like conversational AI that maintains coherence and context across extended exchanges.
